---
title: "Research"
toc: true
---

My research interests lie at the intersection of **systems**, **security**, and **machine learning**. I am drawn to problems where a principled theoretical understanding can lead to practical, deployable solutions.

---

## Research Areas

<div class="research-area">
<h3>🔒 Systems Security</h3>
Designing and analyzing secure distributed systems, with a focus on adversarial robustness and formal verification of security properties. I am particularly interested in how modern system architectures can be hardened against both passive and active adversaries.
</div>

<div class="research-area">
<h3>🌐 Distributed & Networked Systems</h3>
Understanding the fundamental limits of distributed computation and communication. This includes fault tolerance, consensus protocols, and the design of scalable, reliable infrastructure for modern applications.
</div>

<div class="research-area">
<h3>🤖 Applied Machine Learning</h3>
Applying machine learning techniques to systems problems — including anomaly detection in network traffic, predictive resource management, and automated vulnerability discovery. I am especially interested in the reliability and trustworthiness of ML-based systems.
</div>

<div class="research-area">
<h3>📊 Data-Driven Experimentation</h3>
Developing rigorous methodologies for evaluating systems at scale. This includes reproducible benchmarking, statistical analysis of experimental results, and open science practices.
</div>

---

## Collaborators & Affiliations

I have had the privilege of collaborating with researchers at several institutions. I believe strongly in open and reproducible science, and I strive to make my code and datasets publicly available where possible.

---

## Open Source

Much of my research produces open-source artifacts. You can find them on my [GitHub profile](https://github.com/alefiyahussain).
